posts.title: Forced into a Care Home: This Could Have Been Me
posts.author: Sarah Rose
posts.excerpt: Sarah Rose writes about independent living, support from carers and her fear that both could be taken away from people with complex disabilities.

posts.title: Hearing Patient and Carer Voices, Part 1 — From Visiting to Presence: Why ‘Partners in Care’ Must Be the Future
posts.author: Garry Perry
posts.excerpt: Garry Perry describes why lived experience and co-design should shape care across the Royal Wolverhampton and Walsall NHS trusts.

posts.title: Hearing Patient and Carer Voices, Part 2 — Recognising Carers: Making ‘Partners in Care’ Real Every Day
posts.author: Zoe Christoffersen
posts.excerpt: Zoe Christoffersen explains how recognising carers as partners can become part of everyday practice.
